metadata.rationale: mission-critical code and exploit-oriented work should use the slower cold CPU lane so it does not compete with the GPU hot-path orchestrator

blocked_reasons: EDB-32586 PoC requires exact AD LDAP server version and patch level to confirm exploitability; version not yet fingerprinted.
No foothold or credentials available; all actions must remain unauthenticated.
No SMB share or RPC user enumeration succeeded, blocking lateral movement or credential harvesting paths.
AXFR failure prevents domain-wide DNS enumeration needed for full hostname inventory.

candidate_actions.rationale: EDB-32586 PoC targets unauthenticated LDAP username enumeration, and anonymous LDAP access is confirmed (6 naming contexts observed).
candidate_actions.title: Refine LDAP enumeration query to target username enumeration

candidate_actions.rationale: IIS version (10.0) is inferred but not confirmed; module/fingerprint details could reveal additional attack surface (e.g., WebDAV, ASP.NET).
candidate_actions.title: Verify IIS version and modules via HTTP fingerprinting

candidate_actions.rationale: Port 88 is open; Kerberos pre-auth enumeration can yield valid usernames without credentials.
candidate_actions.title: Attempt Kerberos pre-auth enumeration via AS-REQ (if Kerberos port 88 is accessible)

capability_gaps: No unauthenticated LDAP version fingerprinting primitive available to confirm AD LDAP server version for EDB-32586 relevance.
No Kerberos pre-auth enumeration primitive available to test AS-REQ against port 88.
No HTTP module fingerprinting primitive available to detect WebDAV/ASP.NET extensions on IIS.
facts: TCP service discovery confirmed open AD-related ports: 389/ldap, 636/ldaps, 3268/globalcatLDAP, 3269/globalcatLDAPssl, 88/kerberos, 464/kpasswd5, 139/netbios-ssn, 445/microsoft-ds, 5985/winrm.
HTTP probe returned IIS Windows Server (200 OK), consistent with AD-integrated web front.
Anonymous AD enumeration observed 6 LDAP naming contexts, indicating AD is present and accessible over unauthenticated LDAP (port 389).
DNS enumeration returned 5 records, but AXFR failed, limiting hostname discovery.
Web content discovery (420-word list, 4 URLs) found no interesting paths.
Public PoC EDB-32586 targets 'Microsoft Active Directory LDAP Server - Username Enumeration' and matches observed LDAP service (port 389).
No SMB shares or RPC users were identified in anonymous enumeration.
stop_conditions: Do not attempt credential spraying or brute force.
Do not execute any PoC code or imply exploitation occurred.
All actions must remain unauthenticated and bounded.
No DoS, flooding, or destructive primitives allowed.
ai_proposal.summary: Target pirate.htb (10.129.48.145) shows an Active Directory-integrated IIS server with standard AD ports open (389, 636, 3268, 3269, 88, 464), but no exploitable surface confirmed. Public PoC for AD LDAP username enumeration (EDB-32586) matches query and service inventory, but exact version and authentication posture are unverified.

metadata.provider_rationale: mission-critical code and exploit-oriented work should use the slower cold CPU lane so it does not compete with the GPU hot-path orchestrator

task.summary: Search local exploit references for evidence-backed services on pirate.htb.
task.title: Research relevant public PoCs
